what_it_does: Documents a gap analysis between a 70-piece mockup estate and a 160-page source corpus, naming underused design registers, confirmed identity patterns, and build-status items for future pieces.
observations: Frames the mockup estate as deck-first while the corpus also shows product and brochure registers.
Identifies missing or thin areas: brochure/marketing layouts, product-mode chrome, evolving diagram systems, multi-page mechanics, and A4/web-scroll scale work.
Lists completed pieces for dashboard, comparison matrix, transforming motif, and gen4-strip, with web-scroll and build-pair still open.
Uses design-substrate terms such as phase strips, stage cards, glass pills, frost cards, role-hues, tangle-to-hub, and metal-axis time.
States that the widened registries and generators expanded the language rather than only adding notes.
standouts: Connects design gaps to concrete artifact types to build.
Separates corpus-confirmed existing strengths from underused registers.
Tracks completed and remaining work with explicit artifact names.
conventions: Uses sectioned markdown with lettered gap categories and status-marked checklist items.
Mixes evaluative design language with implementation-style artifact IDs.
Uses bold emphasis for register names and key substrate terms.
Maintains a ledger tone while retaining source-voice phrases from the assessed material.
concerns: Some referenced terms and artifact IDs require outside context to decode from this file alone.
The status section updates earlier gap claims, so readers must reconcile the initial 'to build' language with later completed markers.
notes: A design assessment ledger that translates corpus evidence into missing registers, confirmed patterns, and named build targets.
questions: What exact artifacts do gen4-strip, gen4-matrix, gen4-motif, and scr-dashboard refer to?
What are the definitions of the referenced registries such as organisms-2, grammar-10, modes-3, and application-3?
What does coverage 0 mean in the final sentence?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a gap analysis and proceeds to enumerate gaps, confirmations, status, and next build targets.
apparent_themes: Design identity as a reusable language system
Marketing, product, diagram, page-sequence, and scale registers
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn corpus ingestion into a practical ledger of design substrates the mockup estate should demonstrate or already demonstrates.
novelty: Treats a visual identity corpus as a set of registries, motifs, generators, and artifact-scale obligations rather than only as style notes.
contribution: Contributes a neutral assessment vocabulary for retaining brochure patterns, product chrome, diagram evolution, multi-page mechanics, scale variants, confirmed motifs, and build-status markers in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown assessment file mapping a mockup estate against a source corpus by identifying underused design registers, confirmed identity patterns, completed artifacts, and remaining build targets.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to convert corpus evidence into a file-local ledger of design gaps, proofs, and next artifacts to build.
